remember: Students will be able to recall the key features of the Five-Year Plans in India.
Students will be able to identify the major objectives of planned development in India.
Students will be able to list the important institutions involved in the planning process.
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of planned development in India.
Students will be able to describe the role of the Planning Commission and the National Development Council.
Students will be able to distinguish between the different types of Five-Year Plans implemented in India.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of planned development to real-life scenarios in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the planning process in achieving its objectives.
Students will be able to suggest alternative strategies for addressing development challenges in India.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the planning process in India.
Students will be able to critically evaluate the impact of planned development on different sections of Indian society.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the planning process in India with that of other countries.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the concept of planned development and its significance in the Indian context.
To analyze the role of the Planning Commission and the National Development Council in the planning process.
To evaluate the outcomes of the Five-Year Plans and their impact on Indian society and economy.
board_exam_objectives: To assess the students' understanding of the planning process and its institutions in India.
To evaluate the students' ability to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the planning process.
To test the students' knowledge of the major objectives and outcomes of the Five-Year Plans in India.
